rationale: The cited paper is used in the methods section to justify a concrete design decision: keeping alternative presentation order fixed to avoid unintended fluency effects from display variation. This is a specific functional role connected to the citing paper's core mediator (interface fluency), rather than a generic background mention. Although the citation is not central to the paper's main theory, it meaningfully informs experimental control and implementation.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Deng et al. (2016)'s display-fluency insight to its own interface experiments by fixing the order of alternatives so display arrangement does not confound fluency outcomes. This is a methodological use of the cited work in a new mass-customization setting.
